fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but deeply unprofitable with a high valuation and significant cash burn.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by -233.7%; next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Current ratio 18.47 and quick ratio 17.91 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
Gross margin 44.8% suggests potential for future profitability if scale is achieved.
negatives: Operating margin -1014.0% and profit margin 0.0% show extreme unprofitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $-1.41B indicates a substantial cash burn.
Forward P/E of -356.28 and Price/Sales of 483.74 represent a very high valuation.
Return on equity -37.8% and return on assets -6.3% highlight poor capital efficiency.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile saw its stock jump 7% on May 22nd, following broader positive sentiment in the space sector driven by companies like SpaceX and Firefly Aerospace. This surge in space stocks, including Rocket Lab and Planet Labs, indicates renewed investor interest in the industry. The company's stock movement aligns with a general upward trend in the market, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also scoring weekly wins.
